methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Oklo is a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by Jacob DeWitte and Caroline Cochran, currently valued at $10B+ following $500M+ in total capital raised. The nuclear energy sector comprises a limited peer universe of 11 total comparable companies—3 public and 8 private—including public names trading under OKLO, NNE, and SMR, and private peers such as Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Zap Energy.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable in this case. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers; only one same-stage peer exists within narrow filters, falling short of the minimum three datapoints required for statistical reliability. Expanding the peer set to all seven nuclear energy sector companies would introduce excessive heterogeneity across development stages and business models, rendering an all-sector median potentially misleading and uninformative for Oklo's specific positioning.

The analytical limitations here are substantial. Without a viable triangulation method supported by adequate comparable data, a credible valuation range cannot be derived from the information available. Further analysis would require either broadening methodology (e.g., revenue or subscriber multiples, discounted cash flow) or accepting that Oklo's $10B+ public valuation reflects market pricing in the absence of conventional peer-based support. Key uncertainties center on commercialization timelines and the regulatory environment's impact on the addressable market.
